zh: 从车间到工厂食堂大概有10分钟左右走路路程。
姜海此时优哉优哉的整理工位上的物品，关掉车床电源，在工作记录本子上大笔一挥。
起身，哼着小曲，走向车间出口。
此时姜海脑海中的系统提示音响了一下，视觉系统左下角出现了一个列表
【您周围有一些同事】
【生物列表】
【陈小亮 年龄 19岁 等级1 社会经验 50 技能等级 车间车工5级 职位 钳工 综合评定 1 振华长三角电子厂】
【李国强 年龄 35岁 等级3 社会经验 9780 技能等级 车间车工2级 职位 班组长 综合评定 10 振华长三角电子厂】
这一排数据看的姜海有点眼花
"这系统居然可以查看周围附近的人，就是不知道范围是多少"
"系统，我的屏幕左下方的人物列表范围是多少？"
【宿主您好，您目前的搜索范围是方圆10米，随着等级的提升，此范围会扩大】
【目前列表排序是根据距离远近，您也可以选择其他排序条件】
这个功能倒是不错，姜海暗赞了系统一下，随即就把排序调成等级
此时列表第一位置就出现了一组数据
【张建军 年龄 55岁 等级10 社会经验 24500 技能等级 车间管理2级 职位 车间主任 综合评定 80 振华长三角电子厂】
啊哈，居然周围有领导在，官不小，而且是2级。
这个平民世界技能职称分为五级，五级最小。依次是：
五级（初级）→四级（中级）→三级（高级）→二级（大师）→一级（专家） ，这些等级是需通过培训和等级考核才能认定和提升。
"以后要和这个车间主任打交道了，不知此人是个什么样子的人"
姜海心中想到，偷偷查看四周，不怎么费力的，就发现了车间主任。
张建军，身形挺拔如松，头发花白却梳理得一丝不苟，深刻的法令纹从鼻翼延伸至下颌，
仿佛镌刻着车间数十年运转的轨迹。他常年穿着一套洗得发白的深蓝色工装，
左胸别着金底红字的「振华劳模」徽章——这是他在厂庆35周年时的荣誉。
最引人注目的是那双眼睛：眼角密布鱼尾纹，但目光锐利如游标卡尺的刻度线，
扫过车间时连螺丝的毛刺都无所遁形。
走路时腰板挺直，步伐沉稳，手中总拎着一个褪色的铝合金工具箱，箱角因磕碰凹陷，却被擦拭得锃亮。
此时这位车间主任正和下班路过的工友们打着招呼，时而点头示意，也发现姜海看着他，也朝着姜海微笑了一下。
姜海心头一紧，这位车间主任不简单，那么短时间就能感觉到自己的目光并做出反应。。。
是个老江湖，人精。
谨慎的从车间主任身上移开自己的目光，姜海默默的继续朝出口走去。期间也和一些平日里关系好的工友寒暄一下。
匆匆走向食堂。
一路无话，不一会儿姜海就到达了食堂。
此时食堂里面人已经很多了，姜海估计是他下班磨蹭了一会儿，加上其他几个车间离食堂比较近，所以来的稍微晚一点。
食堂内部宽敞但简陋，长条餐桌排列整齐，天花板吊着几盏节能灯，光线略显昏暗。
空气中飘荡着饭菜的香气和嘈杂的交谈声。姜海走到收银台前，准备购买饭票。
饭票是塑料制成的长方形卡片，表面有凹凸纹理和防伪水印，以防仿冒——这是工厂的独特福利。
员工可凭工牌以7折价格购买：一张10元面值的饭票，员工价只需7元；而对来访者或外部人员，则按原价10元销售。
员工餐基本就是两荤一素，米饭无限添加，正好10元。
姜海取了一份红烧肉、一份酱爆猪肝、炒白菜和米饭，
他默默感叹："比前世公司食堂强多了，之前的公司食堂这一餐怎么也要20块"
姜海端着餐盘找座位时，突然听到有人叫他："姜海，这边！"
抬头一看，是班组长李国强和学徒工陈小亮在靠窗的桌子旁挥手。
李国强身材敦实，脸上带着温和的笑容，作为二级钳工，他在车间里负责监督生产线；
陈小亮瘦高个，一脸青涩，正埋头干饭。
姜海走过去坐下，三人围坐开始闲聊。李国强关切地问："姜海，看你下午在车间脸色苍白，像中暑了？没事吧？"
姜海强装镇定，用原主人的习惯回答："没事，李哥，就有点头晕，可能是这几天没休息"
一边吃饭，一边和李国强他们随意聊天中，姜海发现，这个世界和姜海原世界有所不同。
总体概括来就是：
这个世界真的非常非常平淡。。。
en: The walk from the workshop to the factory cafeteria took about ten minutes.
Jiang Hai took his time tidying up his workstation—switching off the lathe, then grabbing a pen to scrawl something in the production logbook.
He got up, humming a tune, and headed for the workshop exit.
[Ding]
Right on cue, a system chime rang in Jiang Hai's mind. A list materialized in the bottom-left corner of his vision.
[You have some colleagues nearby]
[Biological Signatures Detected]
[Chen Xiaoliang | Age: 19 | Level: 1 | Social EXP: 50 | Skill: Lathe Operator Lv.5 | Position: Fitter | Overall Rating: 1 | Zhenhua Yangtze River Delta Electronics Factory]
[Li Guoqiang | Age: 35 | Level: 3 | Social EXP: 9,780 | Skill: Lathe Operator Lv.2 | Position: Team Leader | Overall Rating: 10 | Zhenhua Yangtze River Delta Electronics Factory]
Jiang Hai's eyes glazed over trying to parse all those stats.
*So the system can detect people nearby? But what's the range?*
*System, what's the detection range for the character list in the bottom-left of my screen?*
[Host, your current search radius is 10 meters. This range will expand as you level up.]
[Current list is sorted by proximity. You may select alternative sorting criteria.]
Not bad, Jiang Hai thought, giving the system mental props. He immediately switched the sort to Level.
The first entry on the list instantly changed.
[Zhang Jianjun | Age: 55 | Level: 10 | Social EXP: 24,500 | Skill: Workshop Management Lv.2 | Position: Workshop Director | Overall Rating: 80 | Zhenhua Yangtze River Delta Electronics Factory]
Well, well—a supervisor was nearby. And not just any supervisor, but one ranked Level 2.
In this world, the skill ranking system had five tiers, with tier five being the lowest:
Tier 5 (Novice) → Tier 4 (Intermediate) → Tier 3 (Advanced) → Tier 2 (Master) → Tier 1 (Expert). Advancement required training and formal assessment.
*Guess I'll be dealing with this workshop director eventually. I wonder what kind of person he is.*
Jiang Hai glanced around—and spotted the director without much trouble.
Zhang Jianjun stood ramrod straight, like a pine tree refusing to bend. His hair was graying but combed back with meticulous precision. Deep nasolabial folds ran from his nostrils to his jawline.
They seemed carved by decades of keeping this workshop running. He perpetually wore a faded navy-blue jumpsuit, its color long washed out from years of washing.
On his left breast gleamed a gold badge with red characters: 'Zhenhua Model Worker'—an honor from the factory's 35th anniversary celebration.
Most striking were his eyes: crow's feet lined the corners, but his gaze was sharp as the markings on a vernier caliper.
When those eyes swept across the workshop floor, not a single burr on a screw escaped notice.
He walked with an upright posture and steady steps, always carrying a dented aluminum tool case—its corners battered from years of knocks, but polished until they gleamed.
The workshop director was currently greeting workers heading home, nodding to each one. He caught Jiang Hai staring and offered him a slight smile.
Jiang Hai's heart skipped a beat. This director was no ordinary man—to sense Jiang Hai's gaze and react in such a short time...
An old fox. A shrewd one.
Carefully averting his eyes from the director, Jiang Hai continued toward the exit, exchanging pleasantries with a few familiar faces along the way.
He made his way to the cafeteria in silence.

Before long, Jiang Hai arrived. The place was already packed—he figured he'd dawdled a bit after his shift, plus a couple other workshops were closer to the cafeteria, so they naturally arrived first.
The cafeteria was spacious but spartan. Long cafeteria tables stood in neat rows. A few energy-saving lights hung from the ceiling, casting everything in dim, yellowish light.
The air carried the aroma of food and the hubbub of chattering workers. Jiang Hai walked up to the cashier's counter, ready to buy meal tickets.
The meal tickets were plastic rectangular cards, textured with raised patterns and anti-counterfeit watermarks—a unique factory perk.
Workers could use their ID badges to purchase tickets at a 30% discount: a 10-yuan ticket cost just 7 yuan for employees. Outside visitors paid the full 10 yuan.
The standard employee meal was two dishes of meat plus one vegetable, with unlimited rice—all for exactly 10 yuan.
Jiang Hai picked up a portion of braised pork, a serving of stir-fried pig liver, some cabbage, and a tray of rice.
He couldn't help but think: *Way better than my previous company's cafeteria. That place would've charged at least 20 yuan for this spread.*
As Jiang Hai carried his tray looking for a seat, a voice called out: "Jiang Hai! Over here!"
He looked up to see Team Leader Li Guoqiang and apprentice Chen Xiaoliang waving at a window-side table.
Li Guoqiang was stockily built, with a warm smile on his face. As a Level 2 fitter, he oversaw the production line in the workshop.
Chen Xiaoliang was tall and lanky, his face still youthful and naive, shoveling food into his mouth with complete focus.

Jiang Hai walked over and sat down. The three of them began chatting as they ate. Li Guoqiang asked with concern: "Jiang Hai, you looked pale in the workshop this afternoon. Like you had heatstroke. You okay?"
Jiang Hai forced himself to stay calm, replying in the manner of his predecessor: "I'm fine, Brother Li. Just felt a bit dizzy. Probably haven't been resting well these days."
As they ate and chatted, Jiang Hai noticed something: this world was different from his original one.
To sum it up in one phrase:
This world was really, really... dull. Unremarkably, frustratingly ordinary.
